hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
expected_string = hidden_string[::-1]
# The measurement result for qubit i is stored in classical bit i.
# Qiskit bitstrings are ordered from highest bit to lowest (q_{n-1}...q_0).
# Therefore, the measured bitstring is the reverse of the hidden_string.
most_frequent_measured = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert most_frequent_measured == expected_string

expected_key = hidden_string[::-1]

```python
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
# Bernstein-Vazirani identifies the hidden string in one query.
# We reverse the hidden string because Qiskit bitstrings are ordered from 
# the highest index qubit (MSB) to the lowest index qubit (LSB).
measured_string = max(counts, key=counts.get)
assert measured_string == hidden_string[::-1], f"Expected {hidden_string[::-1]}, but got {measured_string}"
```

```python
# The Bernstein-Vazirani algorithm results in a measurement of the hidden bitstring.
# Since Qiskit uses little-endian bit-ordering (classical bit 0 is the rightmost character),
# the measured bitstring in the counts dictionary is the reverse of the hidden_string.
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
expected_bitstring = hidden_string[::-1]
assert max(counts, key=counts.get) == expected_bitstring
```

```python
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
# In Qiskit, the bitstring in counts is ordered from highest qubit index to lowest.
# Since qubit i is mapped to classical bit i, the expected bitstring is the reverse of hidden_string.
expected_bitstring = hidden_string[::-1]
assert counts.get(expected_bitstring, 0) > 0.5, f"Expected hidden string {expected_bitstring} not found as the majority result."
```

```python
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
# In Qiskit, bitstrings are ordered from highest index to lowest index (q_n, ..., q_0).
# The oracle encodes hidden_string[i] into qubit i, so the result bitstring is the reverse of hidden_string.
expected_bitstring = hidden_string[::-1]
measured_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)

assert measured_bitstring == expected_bitstring
assert counts[measured_bitstring] > 0.5
```

hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")

expected_bitstring = hidden_string[::-1]
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)

```python
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
most_frequent_outcome = max(counts, key=counts.get)
# In Qiskit, the bit at index 0 is the rightmost character in the count bitstring.
# Since the oracle applies CX from qubit i to the ancilla based on hidden_string[i],
# the resulting measurement of qubit i (bit i) will match hidden_string[i].
# Thus, the full measured bitstring will be the reverse of the hidden_string.
assert most_frequent_outcome == hidden_string[::-1]
```

```python
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
# Qiskit measurement bitstrings are ordered from the highest-index classical bit 
# to the lowest (c_n...c_0). Since qubit i maps to cbit i and hidden_string[i], 
# the measured result should be the reverse of the hidden_string.
expected_result = hidden_string[::-1]

assert expected_result in counts
assert counts[expected_result] == max(counts.values())
```

```python
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
# In Qiskit, bitstrings are ordered from most-significant bit (last qubit) to least-significant bit (first qubit).
# Since the oracle assigns hidden_string[i] to qubit i, and qubit i is measured to classical bit i,
# the resulting bitstring in counts will be the reverse of the input hidden_string.
expected_bitstring = hidden_string[::-1]
most_frequent_bitstring = max(counts, key=counts.get)

assert most_frequent_bitstring == expected_bitstring
assert counts[most_frequent_bitstring] > 0.5
```

```python
hidden_string = metadata.get("hidden_string", "111")
# For Bernstein-Vazirani, the measured result should be the hidden string
# In Qiskit's bitstring representation, qubit 0 is the rightmost character.
# Given the circuit maps hidden_string[i] to qubit i and qubit i to bit i,
# the result in counts should be the reverse of the hidden_string.
expected_string = hidden_string[::-1]

# We verify that the expected bitstring is the most frequent measurement
assert expected_string in counts, f"Expected string {expected_string} not found in results"
assert counts[expected_string] == max(counts.values()), "Hidden string is not the most frequent outcome"
assert counts[expected_string] > 0.5, f"Frequency of {expected_string} is too low: {counts[expected_string]}"
```
